page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading, and 96 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and instruction-level performance. The server achieved a Passmark CPU score of 58,467.72 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,375.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also strong, measuring 276.52 GB/sec for reads and 118.36 GB/sec for writes. In application-specific tests, the instance demonstrated strong throughput in static web serving at 68.79 GB/sec and database operations. However, single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1,862.0 and stress-ng single-core performance of 1,680.69, remain in the middle 50% average tier.

The n4-highcpu-48 offers a resource profile optimized for compute-heavy workloads where memory density is not the primary driver. This design provides qualitative cost efficiency by minimizing memory overhead costs for highly parallelized CPU tasks. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. For instance, while prompt processing for small and medium LLMs performs in the top 25% tier, text generation for large 70B LLM models falls into the bottom 25% tier at 2.71 tokens/sec due to memory and accelerator constraints. The server is best suited for multi-threaded CPU tasks, static web serving, database operations, and intermediate vector or matrix calculations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 96 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 58,467.72 and strong large-block memory bandwidth. It also excels in static web serving and database operations. However, its low memory-to-core ratio limits its efficiency for memory-heavy tasks, as evidenced by weak text generation speeds on large 70B LLM models. This server is optimized for parallelized CPU-bound workloads, database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized x86_64 server with 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring forty-eight dedicated Intel vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for matrix and vector operations
Strong multi-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and parallel CPU tasks
